page.title: About ZeroJackpot – Eurojackpot odds
page.description: Understand Eurojackpot odds. Jackpot chance is 1 in 139,838,160. See the maths and why investing often beats the lottery.
page.ogDescription: Playing once a week, it would take on average almost 2.7 million years to hit the jackpot. Understand the maths.
page.twitterDescription: Eurojackpot jackpot odds are 1 in 139,838,160. Understand the maths.

about.intro1: ZeroJackpot shows the hard truth about Eurojackpot: your chance of winning is almost zero. Many play every week without grasping how low the odds are. This simulator shows thousands or even millions of draws in seconds.
about.intro2: After 10,000 draws you may still have won only small amounts. After 1,000,000 draws (roughly 2,740 years of weekly play) you may still not have hit the jackpot – that is how unlikely a win really is.
about.quote: If you played once a week, it would take on average almost 2.7 million years to win the jackpot.
about.purposeP: We are not here to kill the dream of winning, but to give a realistic picture of probability. We want you to:
about.purposeLi1: Understand the real odds before you play
about.purposeLi2: See the lottery as entertainment, not an investment
about.purposeLi3: See what happens if you put the same money into long-term investing instead
about.purposeLi4: Make informed choices about how you spend your money
about.investP1: The simulator shows what happens if you invest the same €2.50 per week in a global index fund with 7% annual return instead. For most people the gap is shocking – even a small weekly sum grows fast thanks to compound interest.
about.investP2: A line costs about €2.50. Playing every week for 10 years means spending roughly €1,300, often winning only small amounts. Investing that money instead could leave you with over €1,600. After 30 years the gap is even bigger.
about.ejP: Eurojackpot is a European lottery: pick 5 numbers from 1–50 and 2 star numbers from 1–12. There are 12 prize tiers, from the jackpot (5+2) down to the smallest win (2+1).
about.oddsIntro: Here is the probability for each Eurojackpot prize tier:
about.oddsSummary:
The probability of winning the jackpot is 1 in 139,838,160.

The chance of winning anything at all in a single draw is about 1 in 32, or 3.1%. So 96.9% of all lines win nothing.
about.persp1: You are more likely to be struck by lightning (about 1 in 500,000) than to win the jackpot
about.persp2: You are more likely to die in a plane crash (about 1 in 11 million) than to win the jackpot
about.persp3: If you played once every day, it would take over 380,000 years on average to win the jackpot
about.persp4: If all 10.5 million Swedes played one line, statistically only about 0.075 people would win the jackpot
about.mathP1: The number of combinations in Eurojackpot comes from main numbers and star numbers:
about.mathP2: That is why jackpot odds are exactly 1 in 139,838,160. No strategy or system changes this. Each draw is independent.
about.truthP1: As the simulator shows: long term you lose money on the lottery. That is by design. Only about 50% of stakes return to players as prizes; the rest goes to the state, good causes and costs.
about.truthP2: Expected value per line is only about €1.20 even though the line costs €2.50 – so on average you lose about €1.30 per line.
about.gambleP: For some people, gambling becomes a serious problem. If you or someone you know struggles to control play, help is available.
about.helpP2: Stödlinjen offers free, anonymous advice for people affected by gambling and their families. It is never too late to seek help.

sim.investDesc: If you had invested {{amount}} per day for {{years}} years in a global index fund with 7% annual return:

q1.question: What are the odds of winning Eurojackpot?
q1.answer: The jackpot odds are 1 in 139,838,160. Playing once a week, it would take on average almost 2.7 million years to win the jackpot.

q2.question: What is the chance of winning anything in Eurojackpot?
q2.answer: The chance of any win in one draw is about 1 in 32, or 3.1%. So 96.9% of lines win nothing.

q3.question: How much does a Eurojackpot line cost?
q3.answer: A line costs about €2.50 in many countries. On average you get back only about €1.20 per line, so you lose about €1.30 per line played.

q4.question: What if I invest instead of playing?
q4.answer: Playing weekly for 10 years costs roughly €1,300 with usually only small wins. Investing the same in a global index fund at 7% could give over €1,600. After 30 years the gap is much larger.
